title: vector - a list that grows
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A std::vector is a resizable list; push_back adds to the end and size() counts it.
explanation: Vectors are the everyday C++ container - an array that resizes itself. You add to it with push_back and ask how many it holds with size().

hints: v[0] is the first item, indexing from zero.

exercises.question:
Which method adds a value to the end of a vector?

v._(7);
hints: Two words joined by an underscore.
